title: Remove dead event handler _on_alertmanager_config_changed (never observed; references non-existent class)
summary: src/charm.py:278-280 defines _on_alertmanager_config_changed, but nothing ever wires it up: there is no self.framework.observe(...) for it anywhere in the repo. Its docstring also references :class:`AvalancheAlertmanagerConfigChanged`, a class that does not exist in the codebase. The handler is unreachable dead code (the charm has no alertmanager relation). It should be deleted.
why_good_first_issue: Self-contained 3-line deletion with no behavioural impact (the method is never invoked). Easy to prove unused with one grep. New contributor learns how charm events are registered via framework.observe and practises safe dead-code removal. Tests already pass and do not reference it, so risk is minimal.
repro_steps: grep -rn '_on_alertmanager_config_changed' src tests -> only the definition at src/charm.py:278 (no framework.observe, no caller). grep -rn 'AvalancheAlertmanagerConfigChanged' src tests -> only the docstring at src/charm.py:279 (class does not exist). Then: export PATH=$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH && tox -e unit.
repro_result: Confirmed at current HEAD. Greps show _on_alertmanager_config_changed is defined only at src/charm.py:278 and is never observed/called; AvalancheAlertmanagerConfigChanged appears only in the docstring (src/charm.py:279) and is defined nowhere. Coverage from `tox -e unit` lists line 280 (the handler body) as Missing -> never executed, i.e. dead. Unit baseline: 42 passed, 1 warning, 2.31s; total charm.py coverage 85%. Deleting the method will not affect any test.
fix_hint: Delete the three lines src/charm.py:278-280 (the def, its docstring, and the pass-through body). No observe() to remove because none was ever added. Run tox -e lint and tox -e unit to confirm green.
notes: Repo has 0 open issues, so self-identified. ops >=2; uses ops.main. Unit env builds and passes (42 tests). Coverage evidence directly corroborates the dead code (line 280 Missing).

title: Fix stale docstring on _update_layer (documents a 'restart' arg that does not exist)
suggested_labels: good first issue, Type: Documentation, Type: Cleanup
summary: src/charm.py:146 defines `def _update_layer(self) -> bool:` (no parameters), but its docstring (src/charm.py:149-150) has an `Args:` section documenting `restart: a flag indicating if the service should be restarted if a change was detected.` There is no `restart` parameter, and the method never restarts the service (restart is handled separately in _common_exit_hook). The docstring is misleading and out of date.
why_good_first_issue: Pure documentation fix in one method: remove the bogus Args section (or correct it to describe the actual no-arg behaviour and the bool return). No logic changes, no test changes. Teaches a newcomer to read code vs docstring carefully and to keep Google-style docstrings honest, which the repo enforces via ruff D rules.
affected_files: src/charm.py:146-172 (_update_layer); specifically the docstring Args block at src/charm.py:149-150 describing a non-existent `restart` parameter
repro_steps: sed -n '146,156p' src/charm.py (or grep -n 'def _update_layer' and 'restart:' src/charm.py). Observe signature `def _update_layer(self) -> bool:` at line 146 and the contradictory `Args:\n  restart: ...` at lines 149-150.
repro_result: Confirmed at current HEAD: src/charm.py:146 = `def _update_layer(self) -> bool:` (zero params) while src/charm.py:149-150 documents `Args:` / `restart: a flag indicating if the service should be restarted if a change was detected.` The parameter does not exist and the method does not restart anything. Unit suite green (42 passed) regardless, since this is documentation-only.
fix_hint: Edit the _update_layer docstring (src/charm.py:147-155): drop the `Args:` section with the phantom `restart` parameter and keep just the summary plus the `Returns: True if anything changed; False otherwise`. Run tox -e lint (ruff D rules) to confirm the docstring still passes.
notes: Repo has 0 open issues -> self-identified. Could be bundled with the dead-handler cleanup into one small 'tidy charm.py' PR, or filed separately. Also note several inline TODOs exist (scrape_interval move to config at :75; remote-write-interval config at :195; block-if-both-relations at :187) but those are feature-sized, not first-issue-sized.
